In 2010, President Obama led efforts to pass the Affordable Care Act, the most sweeping reform to the nation's healthcare system in nearly five decades. In the presidential election of 2016, Republican Donald Trump was elected as the 45th president of the United States, a result viewed as one of the biggest political upsets since the 1948 election.

Trump led the country through the first waves of the COVID-19 pandemic in the United States, which as of December 2021 is estimated to have killed over 900,000 Americans. In 2020, in what was seen as a repudiation of Trump's divisive leadership, Democrat Joe Biden was elected as the 46th president.

On January 6, 2021, supporters of the outgoing President Trump stormed the U.S. Capitol in an unsuccessful effort to disrupt the Electoral College vote count that would confirm Democrat Joe Biden as the 46th president.

Under amendments made on 3 May 2011, elections must be held by 15 July of final year of a term, with the new term beginning on 1 September. To analyze the merging of the two parts of Germany, a branch was opened in Dresden in 1993. Under President Hans-Werner Sinn, its official name was expanded to the “Ifo Institute for Economic Research at the University of Munich” as an expression of the close cooperation between Ifo and the University of Munich (LMU) and especially with the Center for Economic Studies (CES) at LMU, headed by Hans-Werner Sinn.
